clean_text: Alhamdulillah. Continuing in the opening verses of Surah Musl is either 19 or 20 verses. Now a person may ask 19 or 20. How does that work? This is an important point for us to contemplate and that is that the way that the verses are numbered mostly is based on how the prophet sallall.aii wasallam used to recite the Quran to the companions. and most of it is agreed upon. However, distinguishing which verse ends where and which verse begins where, this is an issue that is not completely agreed upon amongst the scholars of the scholars of the Quran. So we don't have a problem with our book problems that plague the other books because their scholarship was not trustworthy. You look at the Torah that was given to Misam the given to the Zab that was given to Benu. They did not follow through with their entrustment. Benu Israel's scholars were no not much better and no better largely than the rest of the ignorant among them amongst them and as a result they dropped the ball with their book. Some of them deliberately altered it, but general general lack of passion and dutifulness is what plagued their books. So you see now that after all these hundreds of years that these books are not agreed upon not just the number of verses but the number of books themselves we don't have versions of the Quran we have one Quran there are versions of understanding that one Quran but even that has been preserved by Allah Subhanahu wa ta'ala. But we don't have printed multiple versions of the Quran. There is one Quran. It has 114 chapters in it. And every single letter of that book is exactly how it was when it was revealed to our prophet sallallah.aii wasallam 1500 years ago. But the previous books, the Torah, the Injil, the Zabur, they were not protected and preserved by their scholars. And Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala blames them largely in the Quran for their lack of dutifulness in preserving those books. So now you have versions of the Bible, versions where some they have extra books in it, entire books, up to 15 or even 20 books difference between the different versions. more than 40 versions of the Bible which is an amalgamation of parts of the Tawra parts of the Zabur and parts of Aljid which is what we have today called the modern Bible we don't have this issue in the Quran in the Quran every letter is agreed upon amongst the scholars however the exact numbers of verses where this verse ends and where this one begins. That's an issue that has been differed upon. So the actual text itself is not differed upon. However, the division of that text into verses there is a difference in the chains of narration and in the ite. So for instance, we'll take a good example of this is at the beginning of fat a verse of fat or is it not a verse? And if it's a a verse is the last verse is that one verse or is that part of the last verse? This is an issue differed upon. This is what's called as the science of counting and determining the verses of the Quran. Is this all one verse or is one verse and the second verse etc. The differences are in this way. And so we know for certain if is part of the Quran is part of the Quran. But is it all part of the same verse or is that two verses is part of the Quran? But is it the first verse of Fat or is it an independent verse on its own or is it just something that is a marking? And so the companions, they did not worry themselves with numbers. They wrote the Quran and they all agreed in those original mah that they distributed in the time of man not to include a single pen stroke that is not part of the Quran. Not a single pen stroke that is not part of the words of Allah. And so everything in those mah is is part of the Quran. How we divide it up that is an issue that is is differed upon. But even that differing is very very slight. You won't find a scholar that says this chapter is 20 verses and another who says this is actually 50 verses. It's not that dramatic. It is merely an issue of where one sentence ends and where the next begins and picks up because as we know there is no punctuation in the Arabic language as it originally was punctuation period commas exclamation po point or mark question mark colons semicolons etc. these things. This is punctuation. This is for English, not in Arabic. Arabic the the language of the actual words themselves carry their punctuation. It's unnecessary. It's redundant and it's unneeded. And so this is why there would be a difference. But it doesn't mean that there is a difference in the actual words of the Quran or in the actual verses. But now this brings us to a very interesting issue. If we understand that the numbers of verses and where one starts and the other ends is something which there is an issue an element of it had an element of opinion to it and it is something that is not agreed upon amongst the scholars nor is it agreed upon with respect to the chains of narration. This now brings into question the whole numerical quote unquote numerical miracles of the Quran, right? You read some books or some interesting fun facts about the Quran. They say look at this verse number in this chapter in this place. It says this and you look at for instance and everyone wants to find their numbers and think that it's there's some kind of connection there. Entire books have been written on this and it's very fun to imagine. We can understand the allure, the attraction people have to it. However, this is not a science of Islam. This never was a science of Islam. This was never something that was that was you know something that was written on or explored in the time of the major scholars of this and the this is something that when was it introduced? It was largely introduced in the last hundred years or so. And it was largely introduced because of people's obsession with numbers in this new materialistic way of seeing the world. And it was largely introduced because the Muslim started to overlook the point of the Quran and start to look at the aspects of it that were not intended to be looked at in that way. is when Allah would say to them something like threw down his staff and it turned into a snake. It was something to entertain them and we need those details in order to be entertained. And that is why when you read in the modern Bible today, what do you read? The most dry details on things, right? There were fish. How many fish exactly did he catch? And what type of fish were they? And what they said and what he said and what he said back. Why? Because they asked their prophets all of these unnecessary questions and their prophets could not withhold knowledge from them. This is how our companions were. And that is why the Quran is concise and dense in its knowledge. Every letter has benefit to us and is of consequence. There's no fluff there. Why is there no fluff? And this is why when they ask about something, we follow their lead and ask and research it. And when they would not ask about something, we leave that door. We don't ask about it because they were more they were far more passionate about this religion than we will ever be. And they did not leave a single stone unturned. Not a single curtain unopened. not a single door that they didn't knock on if it was something that would be of consequence for them or their their lineage or the Muslim that would come after them. So a lot of these and this is why and we'll end with this point. Do you notice that this kind of phenomenon that came out in the last say hundred years or so about the quote unquote alleged scientific miracles of the Quran or the numerical miracles of the Quran etc. There is you know the religion of Allah is always in the middle between the two extremes. There are groups that say no, there are no scientific or numerical miracles in the Quran at all. That is incorrect. And then there are those who all they see when they hear the Quran or read it is as if the the Quran is a book on geography, as if the Quran is a book a book on metallurgy or a book on physics or a book on space exploration. That is not the point of the Quran. And that is not what it was revealed for. It may have elements of that in it, but those are never the center thrust of the Quran. The Quran is our technology to establish a relationship with Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala. It is an instruction manual. It is a map to guide us to Allah's pleasure and away from his hellfire. And it is something that cannot be reached for. It's not something that we're supposed to twist the Quran in order to make it mean that. It's something that makes itself apparent if you were to look at whether the earth is flat or round in the Quran. You know what's interesting about that? The same evidences used to prove that it is round are the exact same evidences used by earlier scholars to prove that it is flat. Exact same evidences. Why is that? summary: This lecture discusses the opening verses of Surah Muzammil and explores the science of verse numbering (Aad al-Ayat) in the Quran. The speaker explains that while the text of the Quran is completely preserved and agreed upon by scholars, there is scholarly difference regarding where verses begin and end—a minor distinction that does not affect the actual words of the Quran. The lecture addresses a critical issue: the modern phenomenon of 'numerical miracles' in the Quran, cautioning that such obsessions are a distraction from the Quran's true purpose. The speaker compares this behavior to how Bani Israel asked unnecessary details about religious matters, missing the actual message and wisdom of their scriptures.
suggested_title: Understanding Quranic Verse Division and Guarding Against Misuse of Numerical Analysis
